content.full_text:
July 20, 1976

Louis P. Bergna
District Attorney of the
County of Santa Clara
70 W. Hedding Street
San Jose, California 95110

Dear Mr. Bergna:

This letter is in response to your inquiry of July 8, 1976, concerning your financial disclosure obligations under the Political Reform Act.

Since you are not a candidate for re-election this year, your first statement of financial interests will be filed pursuant to Government Code Section 87203 and must disclose investments, interests in real property and income. The report, filed on Form 721, will be due within 30 days of your anniversary of assuming office, the date on which your most recent term of office began. It must cover the period from May 5, 1976, the effective date of the legislation subjecting district attorneys to the disclosure requirements of the Act, to your anniversary date.

Government Code Section 87202 does not apply to you because you already held office at the time Section 87200 was amended to include district attorneys. Section 87202 applies only to district attorneys who are elected to office after May 5, 1976. The requirement that persons file statements "within 30 days after the effective date of this article", Section 87202, applied only to persons holding office on the effective date of the Political Reform Act, January 1, 1975.

If you have specific questions concerning the reporting requirements, please contact our staff.

Sincerely,

Nan Hambleton
Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

bcc: Jeanne P.


COUNTY OF SANTA CLARA
OFFICE OF THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY
24 EAST 815 ROAD / SAN JOSE, CA 95110 / 275-0651
70 W. Hedding St.        95110

LOUIS P. BERGNA
DISTRICT ATTORNEY

July 8, 1976

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

As an incumbent District Attorney, I am trying to figure
out what I have to list on my initial financial disclosure
statement.

My lawyer who is assigned to this matter tells me that the
initial statement is controlled by Section 87202 and
includes only "investments and interests in real property."
Is that your interpretation?

I note that your Form 721 includes much more.  Is that due
to the fact that one can use this form for subsequent
reporting, such as anniversary filings?

Please allow me to tell you that I don't envy your job in
having to try to figure out this legislation.

Thank you for your consideration and help in answering my
questions.

Sincerely,

[signature]
LOUIS P. BERGNA
District Attorney of the
County of Santa Clara

LPB:bb

The image provided is a pink-colored sheet of paper with handwritten text. Due to the quality and angle, some parts of the text are partially visible or illegible. However, I can provide a general summary of what appears to be written: --- Dear Mr. Roberts, This letter is in response to your inquiry of July 8, 1976 regarding your request for information about the Political Reform Act. Section 84705 of the Political Reform Act provides that all public officers who are elected or appointed to office by the people of this state shall file an annual financial disclosure statement with the Secretary of State by January 1st of each year. This requirement applies only to officers whose compensation exceeds $25,000 per year. If you have specific questions concerning the reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act, please consult our staff. Sincerely, [Signature] [Handwritten address] [Handwritten phone number] [Handwritten fax number] [Handwritten email address] --- Please note that due to the quality and partial visibility of the handwriting, some details may not be fully accurate.
